mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and relieves financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy rate is in an easing phase.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after negative FEDFUNDS changes than after flat or rising changes, or if the sign of the relation is negative for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation trend, real rates fall and the market prices a later policy response, which should hurt S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above 4% while FEDFUNDS is below 2%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 55% across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals slowing labor income growth and weaker future consumption, which should translate into lower S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not lower in months with rising UNRATE than in months with flat or falling UNRATE, or if the relationship is positive in a rolling 4-quarter window.

mechanism_narrative: A large monthly drop in PAYEMS identifies labor-market deterioration that reduces household income growth and demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls by more than 150k from the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if months with PAYEMS declines above 150k do not have lower-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns dominate triggered events.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in RSAFS indicates weakening consumer and business spending, which reduces revenue expectations and should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative monthly RSAFS growth is not followed by lower median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the association reverses for any 24-month rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in INDPRO captures a supply-side slowdown that compresses corporate output and margin prospects, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO monthly changes do not correspond to weaker forward SPX returns, or if the event-study average return after the trigger is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and RSAFS both rise together, the economy shows synchronized housing and consumption strength, supporting revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in HOUST and RSAFS fail to outperform single-series signals, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive more than 55% of the time in the joint-strength state.

mechanism_narrative: During recession-like conditions, a simultaneous rise in unemployment and weak output marks deteriorating final demand and earnings, so the combination of rising UNRATE and falling GDPC1 should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months and GDPC1 growth turns negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not produce negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 60% in triggered episodes.
